_comment: Railway template configuration as code for hermes-station.
SCHEMA STATUS: best-effort. Railway's authoritative template-config JSON
schema is not publicly documented at the field level, and we don't have an
existing dashboard export to seed this from. The structure below reflects
what the DX plan calls for (single service, Dockerfile builder, /data
volume, service domain on port 8787, IMAGE_REVISION reference variable,
and three env-var declarations with the OPENROUTER_API_KEY demoted to
optional). Field names follow Railway's documented conventions
(serviceDomain, targetPort, healthcheckPath, builder=DOCKERFILE,
dockerfilePath, variables.* with required/description/default/value).
INTENDED USE: this file is meant to be re-exported / round-tripped through
Railway's dashboard 'Export Project Config' (or `railway template export`)
once an authoritative version is available. Treat the current contents as
documentation of intent rather than a guaranteed-importable artifact.
KNOWN UNKNOWNS:
- Exact top-level wrapper Railway expects (e.g. {"services": [...]} vs
{"version": N, "services": {...}}). Using a flat services-array form.
- Whether 'source' should be a string repo URL or an object. Using object.
- Whether reference vars use ${{ }} or $$ delimiter inside template JSON.
Using ${{ }} per Railway's variable-reference docs.
- Whether volumes live at services[].volumes or top-level.
Placing inline on the service for clarity.
name: hermes-station
description: Self-hosted Hermes WebUI + agent + admin in one container.

IMAGE_REVISION.description: Git SHA of the deployed commit; threaded into the build as --build-arg and surfaced on /health.versions.image_revision.

HERMES_ADMIN_PASSWORD.description: Password for the /admin dashboard. Choose a strong value; rotate by redeploying.

HERMES_WEBUI_PASSWORD.description: Password for the WebUI (end-user chat) login.

OPENROUTER_API_KEY.description: Optional — auto-seeds provider on first boot. Skip to configure via /admin after deploy.
